title: leucophae nutans
intro: Leucophae nutans, also known as Blumea lacera, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leucophae nutans include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate skin irritations. Leucophae nutans is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its medicinal use, and it has also been used in traditional remedies for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
